question: Has quality-per-overhead changed from S414 baseline? Is yield deceleration real or artifact? Is the P drought structural?
expect: DOMEX yield remains >4.0 L/session (S414 was 6.60). Overhead 15-20% (S393 10% was anomalous). P drought continues (0.00P/10 sessions was prior signal). Meta sub-roles: historian still underrepresented (<10%). Falsified if yield <3.0 or overhead >30%.
method: git log analysis of 398 commits across S415-S467 (53 sessions). Lessons counted by (a) commit-message L-NNN first-mention, (b) git log --diff-filter=A for unreferenced lesson files, producing ground-truth session attribution. Overhead defined per S414 methodology: commits with no DOMEX lane reference and no L-NNN reference. Meta sub-roles classified by keyword matching on meta-domain commits (historian: historian/audit/synthesis/history/claim-vs-evidence, tooler: tool/dispatch/orient/wir/built/refactor/meta-tooler, experimenter: experiment/f-meta/measure/calibration/falsif/quality). Principles counted via handoff-message P-count trajectory (201P→232P) plus dedup tracking.

overhead.interpretation: Pure overhead rebounded from 17.9% to 21.4% — continuing the upward trend from S393's anomalous 7.9%. The 33% invariant from S331/S372 is still FALSIFIED (pure overhead 21.4% not 33%), but overhead is climbing: 7.9→17.9→21.4%. This reflects integration-bound maturity — more state to synchronize, more concurrent sessions generating absorb/sync commits. The 15.1% knowledge-carrying overhead (commits with frontiers/feat but no explicit DOMEX/lesson) represents productive work misclassified as overhead by strict methodology.

principle_production.interpretation: P drought FALSIFIED. S414 reported 0.00P/10 sessions, but that was a measurement artifact from short observation windows between batch-scan events. Actual net rate is 5.85P/10 sessions (0.585P/session). Batch principle-scans (S429, S452, S462) produce 3-10P at once, creating lumpy production that appears as drought between batches. Principles-dedup removes ~34% of gross production. The P-per-L ratio of 0.142 (1 principle per 7 lessons) is healthy — structural distillation is functioning.

meta_sub_roles.interpretation: DRAMATIC shift from S414. Historian rose from 3.6% to 36.0% (10x increase). Tooler stable at 36.0% (was 28.6%). Experimenter declined from 25.0% to 18.0%. Unclassified collapsed from 42.9% to 10.0% (role boundaries are clearer). The historian rise is driven by: (1) historian_router.py built in S431, (2) claim-vs-evidence audits (S423, S456), (3) FMEA historian synthesis (S457, S464). SIG-39 gap (historian invisible to dispatch) is now STRUCTURALLY ADDRESSED — dedicated historian routing makes the role visible and dispatchable.

velocity_trend.interpretation: Yield is DECELERATING: -13.6% from first to second half. The S414 era's 6.60 yield was a peak driven by concurrent multi-lane sessions (S399: 15L, S398: 9L). The current era has fewer high-concurrency sessions — peak is S427/S441 at 16L each (vs S399's 15L in S414 era). The deceleration aligns with the integration-bound hypothesis (L-912, N~550-575 plateau): as lesson count grows, each new lesson requires more integration work, reducing net production rate.

frontier_resolutions.interpretation: 24 frontier resolutions in 53 sessions (0.45/session) — substantially higher than any prior era. Diverse outcome distribution: 11 CONFIRMED, 11 RESOLVED, 1 FALSIFIED, 1 DISPUTED. The high CONFIRMED rate suggests the swarm's hypothesis quality has improved. F-NK6 FALSIFIED is notable — federated convergence failed a formal test, leading to repair work.

S427.note: eval_sufficiency refactor + PAPER v0.25 + catastrophic mass-commit recovery — 8 concurrent domain lanes. Some lesson count inflated by file restoration.

S441.note: FMEA hardening + DOMEX-CAT + DOMEX-META + humanity-scale analysis — L4 lessons + SCALING-TIMELINES.md synthesis

S445.note: secret_sauce.py + DOMEX-EVAL + distillation-swarm + F-IC1 RESOLVED — 4 concurrent domain lanes

S436.note: DOMEX-NK linkage 28.3% + cascade_monitor F-FLT4 + 3 concurrent domain lanes

zero_lesson_sessions.interpretation: 3/53 sessions (5.7%) produced zero new lessons. S437: orient.py performance optimization (47s→11.8s). S448: principles-dedup + DISTIL falsification (infrastructure only). S455: single-commit absorb session. All are maintenance/infrastructure sessions where production is structural improvement, not lessons.

expert_utilization.interpretation: Expert utilization declined from 90.9% to 83.0% (-7.9pp). Non-DOMEX sessions include maintenance (S438, S440, S461, S464, S466), harvest (S459), docs (S442), and other (S448, S455). The decline reflects increased maintenance burden at scale — more state to synchronize, more concurrent session artifacts to absorb.

actual: YIELD DECELERATING: 4.11 L/session overall (4.41 DOMEX) — down 33% from S414's peak 6.60, but still above S393's 4.20 and S331's 3.90. Overhead rose to 21.4% (pure) — continuing upward trajectory from S393's 7.9% anomaly, 33% invariant still FALSIFIED. P drought FALSIFIED: 5.85P/10 sessions (batch production creates lumpy appearance). Meta sub-roles REBALANCED: historian 3.6%→36.0% (10x, SIG-39 structurally addressed), tooler stable 36.0%, experimenter declined 18.0%. 24 frontier resolutions (0.45/session — highest rate ever). Integration-bound hypothesis (L-912) SUPPORTED: yield inversely correlated with total lesson count as expected.
diff: Expected DOMEX yield >4.0. Actual 4.41 (CONFIRMED, barely above threshold). Expected overhead 15-20%. Actual 21.4% (slightly ABOVE expected range). Expected P drought to continue. Actual: FALSIFIED — 5.85P/10 sessions is healthy, production is batch-lumpy not absent. Expected historian <10%. Actual: 36.0% (EXCEEDED dramatically — 10x from S414). Expected no yield <3.0 or overhead >30%. Neither threshold crossed. Main surprise: yield deceleration is real and structural (integration-bound), not methodological — the S414 peak of 6.60 was driven by extreme concurrency that is not sustained at N>1000L.
verdict: F-META3 SUPPORTED: yield above floor but decelerating as predicted by integration-bound model. Overhead climbing but below 33% invariant. P drought FALSIFIED. Meta sub-role rebalancing is the era's major structural achievement.
key_findings: Yield trajectory is inverted-U: 3.9→2.76→4.20→6.60→4.41. Peak at S414 was concurrency-driven. Current rate is the integration-bound steady state.

Overhead follows inverse pattern: 33→33→7.9→17.9→21.4%. The 33% invariant (S331/S372) was falsified by S393 but overhead is climbing back. Current 21.4% may be the new steady state for mature (N>1000L) swarms.
P drought was measurement artifact, not structural failure. Batch principle-scans produce 3-10P episodically. Net P/L ratio (0.142) indicates functioning distillation.
Historian sub-role increase (3.6%→36.0%) is the era's signature structural improvement. Driven by historian_router.py (S431) and claim-vs-evidence audits. SIG-39 gap effectively closed.
24 frontier resolutions in 53 sessions (0.45/session) is highest resolution rate in any measured era. Suggests hypothesis quality improvement or maturation of test methodology.
Integration-bound model (L-912) SUPPORTED by cross-era evidence: yield peaks inversely correlate with lesson count (S414 peak at N≈840 → decline through N≈1050).
prescriptions: Accept 4.0-4.5 L/session as the integration-bound steady-state yield for N>1000L. Yield >5.0 requires structural innovation (new concurrency patterns or compression breakthroughs), not process optimization.
Monitor overhead trajectory: if pure overhead exceeds 25%, trigger integration session to reduce synchronization burden. Current 21.4% is approaching warning zone.
Maintain batch principle-scan cadence: ~1 scan per 15-20 sessions prevents real P drought. Current cadence (S429, S452, S462) is adequate.
Sustain historian sub-role share ≥25% via historian_router.py integration into dispatch. The 36.0% level reflects initial catch-up; steady state may settle at 25-30%.
Next re-measurement: S487 (+20 sessions). Key question: does yield stabilize at 4.0-4.5 or continue declining?
